release_notes: Auto-updater hardening so a new file in the manifest can never again brick the update path for old clients. Previously _manifest_file_entries raised ValueError if the manifest listed a filename not in UPDATE_ALLOWED_FILES, killing the whole update batch — meaning ANY new auxiliary file we added to the repo would trap every existing direct-installer client until they manually copied the new chairside_ready_alert.py. That's exactly what happened when ready_animations.py was added in 1.0.41: every client running 1.0.40 or earlier got 'Update path is not allowed' and silently stayed on 1.0.40 through six releases. v1.0.48 changes the unknown-filename case to a warning + skip (logged to startup_log.txt) so the rest of the batch still applies — including any chairside_ready_alert.py change that broadens the whitelist. The unsafe-path check (absolute paths, '..', path separators) still hard-fails as a security boundary. No behavior change for animations themselves; this release exists solely to make future updater additions safe.
